sourceMovie.title: Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Batman: Gotham Knight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug clicked for you, Batman: Gotham Knight (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Degeneration, Batman: Gotham Knight, Pokémon: Jirachi - Wish Maker—each shares DNA with Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug?
faq.answer: Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug (1991) is best described as Action, Animation, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Dragon Ball Z Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
